question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released on April 10, 2026, showing a monthly increase of exactly 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for YES resolution. Multiple sources confirm this 0.3% figure, including the official BLS report and financial news outlets. The market was pricing in a 30% chance of exceeding 0.9%, but the actual result makes this impossible since the data is now official.
key_factors: Official BLS data released April 10, 2026 shows 0.3% monthly CPI increase
Result is well below the 0.9% threshold needed for YES resolution
Data release occurred before market close, making outcome certain
Multiple reliable sources confirm the 0.3% figure
risks: Potential data revisions (though highly unlikely for such a large change)
Misinterpretation of seasonal vs non-seasonal adjustment
Market inefficiency in processing the already-released data
